tcd_title: Fabric DVFS RAPL/RACL Limits and ELC Mode
desc: RACL (Running Average Current Limiting) is not applicable on NWP. NWP has a single IMH die (NIO) and single VCCIN rail — unlike DMR which has dual IMH (Root+Leaf) and dual VCCIN (VCCIN0/VCCIN1). RACL exists specifically for dual-VCCIN architectures where per-VR TDC != total TDP and each VR needs independent current protection. On NWP single-VCCIN, socket RAPL is sufficient for TDC management. Additionally, IO Fabric DVFS (TCD 22022420842) and Memory Fabric DVFS (TCD 22022420849) are both ZBB'd on NWP — the DVFS context for ELC Mode and RAPL/RACL Limits is itself not exercised. [val_agent] Rejected for NWP 2026-05-31
rationale: dvfs_focus.py has dedicated ELC functions: elc_sweep, elc_disable, verify_elc_low, verify_elc_high
gaps: Verify NWP ELC register paths are correct (not DMR dual-IMH paths)
enhancement: COVERAGE_GAP: NWP_ADAPTATION — confirm ELC sweep uses NIO (single IMH) register paths

desc:
▶ NWP Negative Stability Test — UFS ZBB Random DVFS Injection
⚠ NWP Scope Change (2026-06-10): Original Solar DVFS test is ZBB on NWP (Solar architecture absent). This TC is repurposed as a negative/stability test: randomly inject UFS_CONTROL TPMI writes while UFS is disabled (UFS_DISABLE fuse=1, mesh fixed at 2 GHz) and verify no crash, hang, or MCA. Solar tool replaced by PythonSV. Original description preserved below.
1. Validation Scope

Validates that NWP system stability is maintained when random Fabric DVFS UFS_CONTROL register writes are injected via TPMI while UFS is Zero Bug Baseline (UFS_DISABLE fuse=1). Primecode (SPMFWREQ-928) must hold the fabric at fused P1 (2 GHz) regardless of written TPMI content.

Negative test objectives:
  • No MCA / IERR / system hang across 100 random UFS_CONTROL writes
  • UFS_HEADER.AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED=1 persists (SPMFWREQ-927 regression)
  • UFS_STATUS.CURRENT_RATIO=20 (2.0 GHz) on both CBBs throughout (SPMFWREQ-928 regression)
  • PLR bit[22] (RACL) stays 0 throughout (ZBB guard)
PASS: All 100 iterations complete; 0 failures; system alive; no MCA.
FAIL: Any ratio deviation, RACL assertion, MCA, IERR, or system hang.
2. Preconditions
  • Platform booted to XOS / SVOS with PythonSV namednodes access
  • NWP silicon: 2 CBBs + 1 NIO die; UFS_DISABLE fuse=1 (customer default, HSD 14024876702)
  • BIOS knobs: all DVFS / UFS knobs at default; no manual UFS enable override
  • No sustained workload driving power above PL2 (avoids confounding PLR bits)
  • PythonSV sv namespace loaded; SVDEFS kernel module loaded
  • Solar tool not required — test uses PythonSV TPMI injection only
3. Test Steps
Step 1 — Gate check: verify UFS ZBB state before injection:
import random, time # NWP CBB TPMI paths cbb0_hdr = sv.socket0.cbb0.base.tpmi.ufs_header cbb1_hdr = sv.socket0.cbb1.base.tpmi.ufs_header cbb0_sts = sv.socket0.cbb0.base.tpmi.ufs_status cbb1_sts = sv.socket0.cbb1.base.tpmi.ufs_status cbb0_ctl = sv.socket0.cbb0.base.tpmi.ufs_control cbb1_ctl = sv.socket0.cbb1.base.tpmi.ufs_control plr = sv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasons # Gate: AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED must be 1 on both CBBs (SPMFWREQ-927) for idx, hdr_reg in enumerate([cbb0_hdr, cbb1_hdr]): val = hdr_reg.read() dis = (val >> 8) & 0x1 # bit[8] = AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED assert dis == 1, 'CBB' + str(idx) + ' AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED=' + str(dis) + ' (expected 1)' print('CBB' + str(idx) + ' AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED =', dis, ' [expected 1]') # Gate: CURRENT_RATIO must be 20 = 2.0 GHz (unit 100 MHz) for idx, sts_reg in enumerate([cbb0_sts, cbb1_sts]): ratio = sts_reg.read() & 0x7F # bits[6:0] assert ratio == 20, 'CBB' + str(idx) + ' pre-test ratio=' + str(ratio) + ' (expected 20)' print('CBB' + str(idx) + ' CURRENT_RATIO =', ratio, ' [expected 20 = 2.0 GHz]')
PASS: Both CBBs AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED=1 and CURRENT_RATIO=20.
FAIL: Either assertion fires — stop; log UFS_HEADER raw value and fuse state.

Step 2 — Inject 100 random UFS_CONTROL writes; verify stability per iteration:
P0_RATIO = 20 # 2.0 GHz / 100 MHz unit Pn_RATIO = 8 # 800 MHz minimum ITERATIONS = 100 failures = [] for i in range(ITERATIONS): rnd_min = random.randint(Pn_RATIO, P0_RATIO) rnd_max = random.randint(rnd_min, P0_RATIO) # UFS_CONTROL: MAX_RATIO[14:8], MIN_RATIO[21:15] ctrl_val = ((rnd_max & 0x7F) << 8) | ((rnd_min & 0x7F) << 15) cbb0_ctl.write(ctrl_val) cbb1_ctl.write(ctrl_val) time.sleep(0.02) # 20 ms = one Primecode slow-loop # Check 1: CURRENT_RATIO must stay at P0 (ZBB enforcement) for idx, sts_reg in enumerate([cbb0_sts, cbb1_sts]): ratio = sts_reg.read() & 0x7F if ratio != P0_RATIO: failures.append('iter=' + str(i) + ' CBB' + str(idx) + ': ratio=' + str(ratio)) # Check 2: RACL PLR bit[22] must stay 0 (ZBB guard) if (plr.read() >> 22) & 0x1: failures.append('iter=' + str(i) + ': RACL PLR bit[22] asserted') if (i + 1) % 10 == 0: tag = 'no failures' if not failures else str(len(failures)) + ' failures' print('Iter', i+1, '/100 -', tag) print('Completed', ITERATIONS, 'iterations. Total failures:', len(failures)) for msg in failures: print(' FAIL:', msg)
PASS: failures=[] after 100 iterations; system responding.
FAIL: Any CURRENT_RATIO deviation or RACL assertion — ZBB enforcement broken in CBB PCode.

Step 3 — Restore UFS_CONTROL to default (0 = driven by fused P0/Pn):
cbb0_ctl.write(0) # 0 = MAX/MIN from fused P0/Pn cbb1_ctl.write(0) time.sleep(0.1) for idx, sts_reg in enumerate([cbb0_sts, cbb1_sts]): ratio = sts_reg.read() & 0x7F print('Post-restore CBB' + str(idx) + ' CURRENT_RATIO =', ratio, ' (expected 20)')
PASS: Both CBBs return CURRENT_RATIO=20 after restore.
FAIL: Ratio does not recover — Primecode/PCode UFS state machine may be stuck.

Step 4 — System stability: MCA / IERR scan:
import subprocess result = subprocess.run(['dmesg', '-T', '--level=crit,err'], capture_output=True, text=True) mca_lines = [l for l in result.stdout.splitlines() if any(k in l for k in ['MCA', 'IERR', 'Machine Check'])] print('MCA/IERR entries found:', len(mca_lines)) for line in mca_lines: print(line)
PASS: 0 MCA/IERR entries post-test.
FAIL: Any MCA or IERR — capture full dmesg and Primecode trace log before reset.
4. Health Check
Collect at test completion regardless of pass/fail:
RegisterNWP PathPurpose
UFS_HEADER (CBB0/1)sv.socket0.cbb{0,1}.base.tpmi.ufs_headerAUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED bit[8] must stay 1 (SPMFWREQ-927)
UFS_STATUS (CBB0/1)sv.socket0.cbb{0,1}.base.tpmi.ufs_statusCURRENT_RATIO bits[6:0] must stay 20 (SPMFWREQ-928)
PERF_LIMIT_REASONSsv.socket0.nio.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.perf_limit_reasonsbit[22]=RACL (must be 0); bit[10]=PKG_PL1_IB (ok if workload running)
pkg_power_consumedsv.socket0.nio.pcudata.pkgRAPLDomain.pkg_power_consumedPower stability monitor during injection
UFS_CONTROL (CBB0/1)sv.socket0.cbb{0,1}.base.tpmi.ufs_controlVerify restore to 0 after Step 3
5. Post-Process
  • failures list with CURRENT_RATIO ≠ 20: CBB PCode UFS ZBB enforcement broken (SPMFWREQ-928 violation) → file sighting against CBB PCode
  • PLR bit[22]=1: RACL activated on ZBB system → file sighting against Primecode TPMI init (SPMFWREQ-927)
  • System hang: capture last HPM 0x1b message via PMSB trackers before reset
  • Confirm UFS_HEADER.AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED=1 persists across all 100 writes
  • Correlate any MCA with iteration number via dmesg timestamp vs script timing
6. HAS Reference
  • NWP IMH SoC PM MAS §6 ZBB Features: nwp_imh_soc_pm_mas.html | HSD 14024876702
  • Primecode FHAS SERVERPMFW-24549: SPMFWREQ-927 (AUTONOMOUS_UFS_DISABLED=1), SPMFWREQ-928 (request fused P1 while UFS_DISABLE=1)
  • DMR Fabric DVFS HAS v1.09: DMR_Fabric_DVFS.html
  • NWP HAS PM Features: NWP_HAS.html#pm-features
  • KB: KB/pm_features/fabric_dvfs/fabric_dvfs_main.md (NWP Delta section)
📄 Legacy Description (Original Solar DVFS TC — DMR)
Original description preserved. The test targeted Solar tool on DMR with dvfs_all_random.xml. Solar is absent on NWP. The negative stability intent has been retained via PythonSV TPMI injection above.

Overview

This test's purpose is to use stress PM DVFS.

Steps:

1 - Boot the system to S0.

2 - Run the Solar script to issue Product Testing using the next XML config file: /usr/bin/solar/solar.sh /cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/DVFS/dvfs_all_random.xml /logpath .

Parameters

/cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/P-DVFS/dvfs_all_random.xml

/logpath .

Prerequisites

System booting SVOS and with Solar installed.

Pass/Fail Criteria

PASS: system completes Product Testing without any fail.

FAIL: the tool result logs any failure.

Triage and Debug

1) Check the log created by the Solar script. Identify the latest flow executing when the platform hung.

2) Further debug depends on the affected area.

tc_title: [Memory Fabric DVFS]  Fabric DVFS Trigger FabricGV  with Pega_silicon
desc:
Flow *

2.2.1 P-state control for Mesh.


Mesh overrride is specified using Module 0 or 1.


Module Range Control
0 IO Mesh frequency
1 Memory Mesh frequency
The PEGA data registers are used as follows for P-state commands:


2.2.2 PEGA Cmd 1 Data Register for P States


Name PEGA Cmd 1 Data Register for P States
Bits Access Default DescripƟon
7:0 RW 0module_maskMask of modules to inject P-states. If bit n is set then the target P-state value for module n will be sent to that module. If bit n is cleared then the target module is excluded from this injecƟon.
15:8 RW 0random_state_targetMask of modules for which random value will be used instead of target P-state specified per module. Note that bits set here will sƟll be qualified based on module_mask in order to determine whether a parƟcular module should be targeted or not.
16:16 RW 0random_module_maskUse random value instead of the value specified in module_mask field.
31:17 RW 0 Reserved
2.2.3 PEGA Cmd 2 Data Register for P States


Note: On DMR, Primecode treats the values for Module 2 and 3 as reserved and ignore those fields


Name PEGA Cmd 2 Data Register for P States
Bits Access Default DescripƟon
7:0 RW 0MODULE0_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 0


11/7/23, 10:40 AM Power Event Generation Architecture (PEGA) HAS [DMR/IMH]


https://docs.intel.com/documents/primecode/has/DMR/PEGA_IMH.html 8/11


15:8 RW 0MODULE1_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 1
23:16 RW 0MODULE2_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 2
31:24 RW 0MODULE3_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 3
2.2.4 PEGA Cmd 3 Data Register for P States


Note: On DMR, Primecode treats the values for Module 4 through 7 as reserved and ignore those fields


Name PEGA Cmd 3 Data Register for P States
Bits Access Default DescripƟon
7:0 RW 0MODULE4_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 4
15:8 RW 0MODULE5_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 5
23:16 RW 0MODULE6_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 6
31:24 RW 0MODULE7_RATIOTarget raƟo to be injected for module 7
An ongoing P-state injecƟon (using the rearm Ɵmer) can be stopped by issuing a new P-state request without the rearm Ɵmer if the user wants to do one final injecƟon or by issuing a new P-state request with no bits set in CMD1.module_mask for all modules to just stop without another injecƟon.


Below is a high level descripƟon of the PCode handling, ucode handling, and pcode->ucode interface for PEGA. Refer to relevant MASes for addiƟonal detail.

Pass/Fail criteria *
